key_insight: High-pressure torsion nanostructures the TiZrHfNbTa refractory alloy and suppresses both thermal and electrical transport, with electrons dominating heat conduction while phonons contribute only a minor share.
engineering_relevance: Severe plastic deformation can be used to tune transport and mechanical properties in refractory high-entropy alloys, which is useful for extreme-environment structural and thermal management applications.
method: High-pressure torsion (HPT) applied to equiatomic TiZrHfNbTa
Measured hardness, specific heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and electrical conductivity versus strain
Interpreted transport using phase analysis, nanograin/dislocation effects, and the Wiedemann-Franz law
evidence_signal: The abstract reports nanograin refinement to about 40 nm, partial BCC-to-omega transformation, and a phonon contribution of only 11–23% to thermal transport depending on strain level.
watch_item: The observed trends are specific to HPT-induced defect structures in this alloy, so the same transport response may not generalize to other processing routes or compositions.
paper_title: Thermal and electrical conductivity of a refractory high-entropy alloy after high-pressure torsion: Electron versus phonon contributions
